**Acceptance Criteria**

Blocking finding: `WorktreeRelocated` warnings are produced but commonly discarded. [worktree_identity.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-d0edc072/crates/jit/src/storage/worktree_identity.rs:107) now makes `load_or_create_worktree_identity()` a wrapper that drops `_warnings`, while most claim/worktree command paths still call that wrapper, for example [claim.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-d0edc072/crates/jit/src/commands/claim.rs:63), [claim.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-d0edc072/crates/jit/src/commands/claim.rs:123), [claim.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-d0edc072/crates/jit/src/commands/claim.rs:262), and [worktree.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-d0edc072/crates/jit/src/commands/worktree.rs:156). Before this change, relocation printed from storage on any of those paths; after this change it only surfaces through `recover` via [claim.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-d0edc072/crates/jit/src/commands/claim.rs:559). This fails “each previously-printed condition still surfaces.”

Executable storage diagnostics were otherwise removed and represented as `StorageWarning` variants in [warnings.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-d0edc072/crates/jit/src/storage/warnings.rs:50).

**Architecture**

Storage no longer directly writes runtime diagnostics to stderr, which fixes the original boundary leak for the updated paths. However, the command layer currently discards storage warnings in common non-recovery workflows, so the output boundary does not consistently own rendering decisions.

**Functional Paradigm**

No new blocking concern. The added warning mapping in [claim_coordinator.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-d0edc072/crates/jit/src/storage/claim_coordinator.rs:198) uses iterator style. Remaining loops are mostly I/O/recovery code where imperative control flow is reasonable.

**Correctness And Invariants**

Atomic writes are preserved for worktree identity via temp-file persist in [worktree_identity.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-d0edc072/crates/jit/src/storage/worktree_identity.rs:270). PID safety is preserved with checked `u32` to `i32` conversion before `kill` in [lock_cleanup.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-d0edc072/crates/jit/src/storage/lock_cleanup.rs:27).

Event logging, gate semantics, assignee format, and label format were not materially changed by this patch.

**Safety**

`#![deny(unsafe_code)]` remains present in [main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-d0edc072/crates/jit/src/main.rs:14). I found no unsafe code introduced in the reviewed diff.

**Testing**

The provided issue context shows `cargo-ci` passed on 2026-06-25T23:21:14Z, so I did not rerun tests. New tests cover warning production for storage internals, but they do not cover the missing propagation through normal claim/worktree command paths, which is the blocking gap.

**Error Handling**

No new library `unwrap()`/`expect()` usage outside tests was introduced in the reviewed changes. Fallible paths continue to return `Result`.

**CLI Contract**

`recover --json` includes structured warnings in [main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-d0edc072/crates/jit/src/main.rs:4751), and human output renders warnings outside storage. The contract is incomplete because non-recover commands that can trigger worktree relocation now silently suppress that warning.

**Documentation**

Public `StorageWarning` has doc comments and examples in [warnings.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-d0edc072/crates/jit/src/storage/warnings.rs:23). Some storage docs still demonstrate caller-side `eprintln!`, but that is documentation/example code rather than runtime storage output.

**Prior Review Feedback**

`run_history` is empty, so there was no prior review feedback to verify.

**Jit Issue Management**

The issue has no dependencies, so there is no prerequisite work to validate. The provided gate evidence has `cargo-ci` passed and `code-review` pending. I did not run `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`; label warnings are allowed, but this review already fails on unmet acceptance criteria.

VERDICT: FAIL
